paragraphs.text: Greetins, i am once again providing new feedback for the digital fairness act. I am writing to express my serious concerns about the proposed Chat Control legislation (CSAM Regulation) currently being reconsidered under the Danish EU Council Presidency. I believe that the proposed legislation is a violation of our fundemental right of privacy. Mass surveiling chat would be incompatible with with Article 7 of the EU Charter of Fundamental Rights: "Everyone has the right to respect for his or her private and family life, home and communications." By trying to push for such initiavite you are betraying the trust of millions of citizen, who woul have their life exposed, giving too much power to agencies across Europe. The breaking of encryption would also expose citizen to potential attacks from cyber-terrorist and organization. This approach will not protect children, rather, the lack of privacy will backfire and cause them to be more expose now than ever. This is a dangerous proposal and would set terrible precedent. This isnt about "finding who could be a threat", but a Trojan horse engineer to spy on the everyday life of hard working citizens and you should be ashamed. As a citizen, i urge for proper democratic scrutiny of this legislation. Sincerely, A concerned citizen.
